Concepts

Agent2Agent (A2A) protocolAdvisorArtifactsAuthenticationData modelDurable in-flight executionFleet streamingLessons (self-reflection)MCP serversOperational notesPer-tenant sandboxSchedulerWeb searchSSE event catalogStructured memoryTenantsTodosWrit

Arbiter CLIOutbound A2A`arbiter --api`Environment`arbiter --init``arbiter --send`Tenant admin

TUI

Arbiter TUISlash commandsKeybindingsPanesSessionsStreaming and turn lifecycleTUI theme JSON

API Reference

Arbiter HTTP API`GET /v1/a2a/agents/:id/agent-card.json``POST /v1/a2a/agents/:id``GET /.well-known/agent-card.json``GET /v1/admin/audit``POST /v1/admin/tenants``GET /v1/admin/tenants/:id``GET /v1/admin/tenants``PATCH /v1/admin/tenants/:id``POST /v1/agents/:id/chat``POST /v1/agents``DELETE /v1/agents/:id``GET /v1/agents/:id``GET /v1/agents``PATCH /v1/agents/:id``POST /v1/conversations/:id/artifacts``DELETE /v1/conversations/:id/artifacts/:aid``GET /v1/conversations/:id/artifacts/:aid``GET /v1/conversations/:id/artifacts``GET /v1/conversations/:id/artifacts/:aid/raw``DELETE /v1/artifacts/:aid``GET /v1/artifacts/:aid``GET /v1/artifacts``GET /v1/artifacts/:aid/raw``POST /v1/conversations``DELETE /v1/conversations/:id``GET /v1/conversations/:id``GET /v1/conversations``GET /v1/conversations/:id/messages``POST /v1/conversations/:id/messages``PATCH /v1/conversations/:id``POST /v1/events``GET /v1/health``POST /v1/lessons``DELETE /v1/lessons/:id``GET /v1/lessons/:id``GET /v1/lessons``PATCH /v1/lessons/:id``POST /v1/memory/entries``DELETE /v1/memory/entries/:id``GET /v1/memory/entries/:id``POST /v1/memory/entries/:id/invalidate``GET /v1/memory/entries``PATCH /v1/memory/entries/:id``GET /v1/memory/:agent_id``GET /v1/memory/graph``GET /v1/memory``POST /v1/memory/relations``DELETE /v1/memory/relations/:id``GET /v1/memory/relations``GET /v1/metrics``GET /v1/models``GET /v1/notifications/stream``POST /v1/orchestrate``POST /v1/requests/:id/cancel``GET /v1/requests/:id/events``GET /v1/requests/:id``GET /v1/requests``GET /v1/runs/:id``GET /v1/runs``POST /v1/schedules``DELETE /v1/schedules/:id``GET /v1/schedules/:id``GET /v1/schedules``PATCH /v1/schedules/:id``GET /v1/schedules/:id/runs``POST /v1/todos``DELETE /v1/todos/:id``GET /v1/todos/:id``GET /v1/todos``PATCH /v1/todos/:id`

Philosophy

Philosophy
Getting Started / Local Install

Local Install

Run arbiter on your own machine. You bring the provider keys; you control the binary. Free, open-source, fully featured. Ten minutes from curl to first agent reply.

Prerequisites

  • A provider key for whichever model family you want agents to use:
  • OpenRouter — OPENROUTER_API_KEY (or save to ~/.arbiter/openrouter_api_key) for hosted models.
  • Ollama — set model ids to ollama/<model> for local models.
  • Ollama — set OLLAMA_HOST if not at http://localhost:11434.
  • Only one is required. Multiple can coexist; different agents can target different providers.
  • Optional: a Brave Search key (ARBITER_SEARCH_API_KEY) if you want /search to resolve. Without it, agents fall back to /fetch on URLs they already know.

Install the binary

macOS arm64

curl -L https://github.com/tylerreckart/arbiter/releases/latest/download/arbiter-macos-arm64.tar.gz \
  | tar xz -C /usr/local/bin

Linux x86_64

curl -L https://github.com/tylerreckart/arbiter/releases/latest/download/arbiter-linux-x86_64.tar.gz \
  | tar xz -C /usr/local/bin

From source (any platform with C++20)

git clone https://github.com/tylerreckart/arbiter.git
cd arbiter
cmake -B build -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release
cmake --build build
sudo cmake --install build

Build deps: OpenSSL, libcurl, SQLite3, a C++20 compiler. libedit or GNU readline is optional but recommended for line editing in the terminal client. Distro-specific install commands are in CONTRIBUTING.md.

First run

export OPENROUTER_API_KEY="sk-or-..."
arbiter --init                          # seed ~/.arbiter/ with starter agents
arbiter                                 # launch the terminal client

--init writes nine starter agents into ~/.arbiter/agents/*.json (reviewer, research, writer, devops, planner, backend, frontend, marketer, social) and is safe to re-run — existing files are preserved unless you pass --force. See cli/init.md for the full layout.

arbiter opens the interactive TUI. Type a message and the master agent (index) decides whether to answer directly or delegate. Switch agents with /use <name>; send a one-off to a specific agent with /send <name> <message>. Full TUI reference: tui/.

Try one-shot mode

For scripts and CI hooks:

arbiter --send reviewer "review: if (arr.length = 0) return;"

The agent runs the full orchestration loop internally — tool calls, sub-agent delegation, advisor gating — and prints the final reply on stdout. See cli/send.md.

Run as an HTTP server

If you want other clients (web UIs, automations, mobile) to drive arbiter:

arbiter --add-tenant me                       # prints the bearer token — save it
arbiter --api --port 8080                     # bind 127.0.0.1 by default

Authenticate with Authorization: Bearer <token>. The endpoint catalogue is at api/. Production deployments belong behind a reverse proxy for TLS and rate limiting — see concepts/operations.md.

Where things live

~/.arbiter/
├── agents/*.json          agent constitutions (edit these)
├── openrouter_api_key     OpenRouter key (alternative to env var)
├── tenants.db             tenant identities for --api
├── sessions/              per-cwd TUI session snapshots
├── memory/                per-agent persistent scratchpads
├── mcp_servers.json       optional MCP server registry
└── a2a_agents.json        optional remote A2A agent registry

Full reference: cli/environment.md.

Next steps

  • Author your own agent. Drop a JSON into ~/.arbiter/agents/<id>.json. Schema: api/agents/create.md. Concepts: Writ, Advisor.
  • Read the design philosophy. philosophy.md.
  • Wire up integrations. MCP servers for external tools, A2A for remote agents.

Arbiter is experimental open-source infrastructure. Philosophy
